title: Dog Crusty Sores Around Eyes: Causes and Triage Advice
meta_desc: Find out why your dog has crusty sores around their eyes, if it is an emergency, and how to help your vet with a photo.
content_html:
What are Crusty Sores Around the Eyes?

Crusty sores around a dog's eyes, often referred to as periocular dermatitis or blepharitis, are typically a sign of underlying irritation, infection, or inflammation. These sores may appear as red, thickened skin that eventually develops a yellow or brown crust due to dried discharge. Common causes include bacterial or fungal infections (like ringworm), skin mites (mange), or environmental allergies that cause the dog to rub their face, leading to secondary skin trauma.

Is it an Emergency?

The urgency level for crusty sores around the eyes is classified as Medium. While this condition is rarely a sudden life-threatening emergency, the eyes are highly sensitive and inflammation can quickly lead to secondary corneal ulcers or permanent scarring if the dog continues to scratch the area. You should aim to see a veterinarian within 24 to 48 hours for a proper diagnosis and treatment plan. If you notice the eyeball itself looking cloudy or if your dog is squinting excessively, seek care sooner.

Why a Photo Helps Triage

Taking a clear, high-resolution photo of your dog's eyes can be a vital tool for your veterinary team. A photo captures the exact state of the sores at a specific moment, which is helpful because clinical signs can change or worsen rapidly. It allows the vet to see the distribution of the crusting and the level of inflammation before the dog arrives at the clinic, and it serves as a baseline to track whether the condition is improving or deteriorating after treatment begins.

faqs.question: Can I use over-the-counter human eye drops on my dog?
faqs.answer: No, you should never use human medications or eye drops on your dog without a veterinarian's approval, as some ingredients can be toxic or worsen certain eye conditions.

faqs.question: How can I safely clean the crusts at home?
faqs.answer: You can gently soften the crusts using a clean, soft cloth dampened with lukewarm water. Do not scrub, use soap, or pick at the sores, as this can cause significant pain and further trauma to the skin.

faqs.question: Are these crusty sores contagious to other pets or humans?
faqs.answer: It depends on the underlying cause. While allergies are not contagious, infections such as ringworm or certain types of mange mites can be transmitted to other animals and occasionally to humans.
